advocacy_topics.name: Increase School Breakfast Funding
advocacy_topics.description: Support legislation that expands access to healthy, free or reduced-price school breakfasts for children in low-income communities.

advocacy_topics.name: Protect and Expand Child Nutrition Programs
advocacy_topics.description: Urge lawmakers to protect and strengthen core child nutrition programs such as SNAP, WIC, and national school meals.

advocacy_topics.name: Strengthen Summer Meals Programs
advocacy_topics.description: Advocate for better funding and flexibility for summer meal sites so children don’t go hungry when school is out.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Summer Meals 2025
fundraising_campaigns.description: Help ensure children have consistent access to nutritious meals all summer long. This peer-to-peer campaign empowers supporters to create personal fundraising pages to keep kids fed when school cafeterias are closed.

fundraising_campaigns.name: School Breakfast Expansion 2026
fundraising_campaigns.description: Fuel young minds by expanding universal school breakfast programs in high-need districts.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Emergency Food Boxes 2026
fundraising_campaigns.description: Provide rapid-response food boxes to families facing sudden crises, disasters, or displacement.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Maternal & Infant Nutrition 2026
fundraising_campaigns.description: Improve nutrition for pregnant women, new mothers, and infants during the first 1,000 days of life.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Holiday Hunger Match 2025
fundraising_campaigns.description: Every dollar given during the holiday season was doubled by generous matching partners to fight child hunger.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Back-to-School Lunches 2025
fundraising_campaigns.description: Equip schools with the resources they need to provide nutritious lunches as children return to classrooms.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Global Child Nutrition Week 2026
fundraising_campaigns.description: A focused week of action to fund innovative child nutrition programs across multiple regions.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Community Pantry Partners 2026
fundraising_campaigns.description: Support local pantries that provide weekend and evening meals to children and their families.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Africa School Meals Drive 2026
fundraising_campaigns.description: Raise vital funds for large-scale school meal programs across multiple African countries.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Central America Nutrition Relief 2026
fundraising_campaigns.description: Provide nutritious meals and supplements to children affected by drought and displacement in Central America.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Weekend Backpack Program 2026
fundraising_campaigns.description: Fill backpacks with nutritious food so children have enough to eat over weekends and school breaks.

fundraising_campaigns.name: Summer Meals 2026
fundraising_campaigns.description: Build on the success of previous summers to reach even more children with daily nutritious meals.

regions.name: Africa
regions.description: Countries across sub-Saharan and North Africa where we support school meals, emergency food, and nutrition programs.

regions.name: Central America
regions.description: Central American countries where children face high rates of food insecurity and chronic malnutrition.

regions.name: North America
regions.description: Programs in the United States, Canada, and Mexico, including school meals and family food support.

regions.name: Asia
regions.description: Nutrition and school feeding initiatives across South, Southeast, and East Asia.

regions.name: Global
regions.description: Cross-regional initiatives that span multiple continents or are not limited to a single region.

children.shortBio: María loves reading stories and wants to become a teacher. Her family often struggles to afford enough food, especially during the lean season.
Diego enjoys playing soccer with his cousins. A sponsorship will help ensure he gets a nutritious meal each school day.
Ana Sofía is shy at first but loves to sing once she feels comfortable. School meals help her focus in class.
Luis loves math and dreams of becoming an engineer. His family’s income is seasonal, making food insecurity a constant worry.
Camila helps her mother sell fruit in the market. Sponsorship would ease the burden on her family and support her schooling.
Javier is energetic and curious. His parents worry about giving him enough nutritious food in his early years.
Amina’s family has been displaced by drought. A sponsorship helps her receive daily meals at a nearby learning center.
Samuel walks several kilometers to school. School meals give him the energy to learn and play.
Naomi dreams of becoming a nurse. Her school participates in a feeding program supported by sponsors like you.
Rahul loves science and building small gadgets. Regular school meals help him stay focused throughout the day.
Farida was recently sponsored and now receives nutritious meals and school supplies.
Jamal participates in a weekend backpack program that sends food home for his family.
Elena represents many children in our global programs who are still waiting for support.

programs.name: School Lunches
programs.shortDescription: Provide daily nutritious school lunches so children can learn, grow, and thrive.
programs.longDescription: Our School Lunches program partners with local schools and communities to provide reliable, nutritious midday meals. For many students, this is the most substantial meal of the day. Each meal is designed with local nutritionists to include protein, whole grains, and fresh produce when available. Your support helps reduce hunger, improve attendance, and boost academic performance.
impactStories: In Guatemala, school attendance rose by 18% after lunches were introduced.
In rural Kenya, teachers report students are more attentive in afternoon classes.

programs.name: Emergency Food Boxes
programs.shortDescription: Rapid-response food boxes for families facing sudden crises.
programs.longDescription: When disaster, conflict, or economic shock strikes, children are often the first to feel the impact of hunger. Our Emergency Food Boxes program delivers shelf-stable staples, fortified foods, and child-friendly snacks directly to families. Boxes are tailored to local diets and can sustain a family for up to two weeks while longer-term support is organized.
impactStories: Following flooding in Bangladesh, emergency boxes reached affected families within 72 hours.
In Honduras, food boxes bridged the gap for families after hurricane damage destroyed crops.

programs.name: Maternal Nutrition
programs.shortDescription: Support nutrition for pregnant women, new mothers, and infants.
programs.longDescription: The first 1,000 days—from pregnancy to a child’s second birthday—are critical for healthy growth. Our Maternal Nutrition program provides fortified foods, micronutrient supplements, nutrition counseling, and breastfeeding support. By improving maternal health, we protect children from the long-term consequences of malnutrition.
impactStories: In Ethiopia, mothers in the program were 35% less likely to have underweight newborns.
Community health workers now use mobile tools to track infant growth regularly.

programs.name: Ethiopia School Meal Program
programs.shortDescription: Nutritious daily meals for students in drought-affected communities of Ethiopia.
programs.longDescription: In partnership with local education authorities, this program provides hot school meals to children in food-insecure districts of Ethiopia. Meals are prepared by community volunteers using locally sourced grains and legumes. School gardens supplement the menu with fresh vegetables, providing both food and learning opportunities.
impactStories: Enrollment among girls increased as families saw the value of daily school meals.
Local farmers benefit from stable demand for their crops.

programs.name: Kenya School Breakfasts
programs.shortDescription: Early-morning porridge so children don’t start school on an empty stomach.
programs.longDescription: Many students in rural Kenya travel long distances and arrive at school hungry. This program provides a fortified porridge breakfast served before classes begin. By ensuring children start the day nourished, we see improvements in punctuality, attendance, and concentration.
impactStories: Teachers report fewer early-morning fainting episodes since breakfasts began.
Parents say children are more eager to attend school knowing a meal awaits.

programs.name: Somalia School Meals & Safe Spaces
programs.shortDescription: Meals and safe learning spaces for children displaced by conflict and drought.
programs.longDescription: In Somalia, cycles of drought and conflict have uprooted millions of families. Our program combines school meals with child-friendly spaces that offer learning and psychosocial support. Nutritious lunches are a powerful incentive for parents to enroll and keep their children in school.
impactStories: In Baidoa, the program helped reopen three schools that had previously closed.
Parents report that children are more active and engaged at home.

programs.name: Guatemala Healthy School Snacks
programs.shortDescription: Fortified snacks and nutrition education for primary students in Guatemala.
programs.longDescription: This program delivers fortified corn-soy snacks and fresh fruit to rural schools in Guatemala. Children also participate in age-appropriate nutrition lessons and school garden activities. The combination of food and education helps address both immediate hunger and long-term health.
impactStories: Stunting rates have begun to decline in participating communities.
Teachers use harvests from school gardens in classroom cooking demonstrations.

programs.name: Honduras Emergency Food Boxes
programs.shortDescription: Emergency rations for families hit by storms and crop loss in Honduras.
programs.longDescription: Extreme weather has disrupted harvests for many smallholder farmers in Honduras. Our emergency boxes include staples like beans, rice, oil, and fortified flour, along with child-friendly items such as milk powder and peanut paste. Local partners help identify the most vulnerable families and deliver boxes quickly.
impactStories: After recent storms, emergency boxes prevented families from resorting to high-interest loans for food.
Community volunteers assist with distribution, strengthening local networks.

programs.name: U.S. Weekend Backpack Program
programs.shortDescription: Send backpacks filled with food home with children every Friday.
programs.longDescription: In many U.S. communities, children rely on free or reduced-price school meals and have little to eat on weekends. Our Weekend Backpack Program discreetly sends bags of child-friendly, easy-to-prepare food home each Friday. School counselors and teachers identify students most at risk of weekend hunger.
impactStories: School staff report improved Monday attendance among participating students.
Parents express relief knowing there will be food at home over the weekend.

programs.name: India Child Nutrition Cash Transfers
programs.shortDescription: Digital cash transfers so families can buy the nutritious food they need.
programs.longDescription: This innovative program provides targeted cash support to low-income families in India to improve child nutrition. Transfers are sent via mobile wallets and are accompanied by nutrition counseling and market monitoring to ensure families can access healthy foods at fair prices.
impactStories: Families report being able to purchase more fresh fruits and vegetables.
Mothers say the flexibility of cash helps them respond to children’s changing needs.

programs.name: Yemen Emergency Child Nutrition
programs.shortDescription: Life-saving therapeutic food for severely malnourished children in Yemen.
programs.longDescription: Ongoing conflict and economic collapse have left millions of children in Yemen acutely malnourished. This program provides ready-to-use therapeutic food, medical screening, and follow-up care. Outreach teams travel to remote villages to identify and treat children before it is too late.
impactStories: Thousands of children have recovered from severe acute malnutrition through this program.
Community health volunteers are trained to spot early warning signs of malnutrition.

fundraising_events.title: Chicago Family 5K – Lakefront Run
fundraising_events.description: Bring the whole family to our Lakefront Family 5K in Chicago. Walk, jog, or run together to raise funds for summer meals for kids across the city.

fundraising_events.title: Downtown Chicago Family 5K & Kids Dash
fundraising_events.description: A festive family 5K and kids dash through downtown Chicago to support weekend backpack programs for local students.

fundraising_events.title: Chicago 10K for Hunger
fundraising_events.description: Competitive and recreational runners take on a 10K course along the river to fight child hunger in the Midwest.

fundraising_events.title: NYC Family 5K in Central Park
fundraising_events.description: Families from across New York City gather in Central Park for a relaxed 5K to support school breakfast expansion.

fundraising_events.title: NYC Riverside Run/Walk for Kids
fundraising_events.description: Run or walk along the Hudson River to raise funds for emergency food boxes in Caribbean and Central American communities.

fundraising_events.title: Los Angeles Sunset Family 5K
fundraising_events.description: An evening lakeside 5K in Los Angeles featuring music, kids activities, and a community fair to support summer meals.

fundraising_events.title: San Francisco Bay Trail Run/Walk
fundraising_events.description: Enjoy scenic bay views while raising funds for weekend backpack programs in Bay Area schools.

fundraising_events.title: Global Virtual 5K for School Meals
fundraising_events.description: Participants around the world log 5K in their own neighborhoods to support global school meal programs.

fundraising_events.title: Chicago Harvest Gala
fundraising_events.description: An elegant evening gala featuring dinner, live music, and an auction to benefit maternal and infant nutrition programs.

fundraising_events.title: Denver Community Meal-Packing Day
fundraising_events.description: Families, corporate teams, and volunteers gather to pack 100,000 meals for children in Central America.

fundraising_events.title: Virtual Info Night: Summer Meals 2026
fundraising_events.description: A live-streamed info session for supporters who want to learn more about the Summer Meals 2026 campaign and how to host a fundraiser.

fundraising_events.title: Atlanta Neighborhood Block Party for Kids
fundraising_events.description: A family-friendly block party with music, games, and food trucks raising funds for school meal programs in Africa.

volunteer_registrations.comments: One youth volunteer is 14 years old and can lift light boxes.
Happy to help with check-in or cleanup.
Group from Lakeside Community Church youth program.
Prefer roles involving direct interaction with families.
Youth volunteer is 16 and has food handler training.

volunteer_opportunities.title: San Francisco Warehouse Meal Packing (Morning Shift)
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help pack nutritious shelf-stable meals for local children and families. Volunteers will work on an assembly line to portion, seal, and box meals for distribution to nearby schools and community partners.

volunteer_opportunities.title: San Francisco Meal Packing (Afternoon Shift)
volunteer_opportunities.description: Join our afternoon shift to assemble meal kits for children who rely on school meals during the week. Tasks include labeling, packing, and staging boxes for delivery trucks.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Oakland Warehouse Sorting & Packing
volunteer_opportunities.description: Sort donated food, check expiration dates, and help pack bulk items into family-sized portions for East Bay school pantries.

volunteer_opportunities.title: NYC School Pantry Distribution
volunteer_opportunities.description: Support a school-based pantry near Penn Station by setting up tables, distributing food to families, and restocking shelves.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Los Angeles Community Kitchen Prep
volunteer_opportunities.description: Assist kitchen staff with chopping vegetables, preparing snack packs, and cleaning to support after-school meal programs.

volunteer_opportunities.title: San Francisco Family Meal Packing (Family-Friendly)
volunteer_opportunities.description: A family-friendly shift where adults and kids work together to assemble meal kits for weekend backpack programs in SF schools.

volunteer_opportunities.title: San Francisco Office Support for Volunteer Services
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help our team with data entry, filing volunteer waivers, and preparing name badges for upcoming events.

volunteer_opportunities.title: San Jose Meal Packing for Summer Meals
volunteer_opportunities.description: Pack meal boxes that will be delivered to summer meal sites across Santa Clara County.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Oakland School Pantry Restocking
volunteer_opportunities.description: Restock shelves, prepare family food bags, and help clean up at a busy school pantry in Oakland.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Chicago Meal Packing for Family 5K Participants
volunteer_opportunities.description: Partner opportunity where runners and supporters pack post-race snack kits for children in local after-school programs.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Denver Corporate Meal Packing Day
volunteer_opportunities.description: Corporate and community teams work side by side to pack 50,000 meals for Central American school programs.

volunteer_opportunities.title: San Francisco Warehouse Inventory Support
volunteer_opportunities.description: Assist staff with counting inventory, updating labels, and organizing pallets to prepare for upcoming meal-packing shifts.
